original_abstract:
Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) has emerged as a cornerstone technology for 5G-Advanced and 6G ecosystems, offering significant improvements in spectral efficiency and hardware integrated costs. By merging radar-like sensing capabilities with wireless data transmission, ISAC supports the rising demand for intelligent applications such as autonomous driving, extended reality, and smart manufacturing. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of ISAC signals, categorized into three core technical pillars: signal design, signal processing, and signal optimization.

We first examine ISAC signal design through the lens of mobile communication evolution, detailing waveforms based on Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) and its derivatives, as well as emerging 6G candidates like Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) and Filter-Bank Multi-Carrier (FBMC). Second, the survey delves into robust signal processing methodologies, specifically focusing on channel information matrix techniques, spectral line estimation, and high-resolution subspace-based approaches for accurate parameter estimation. Third, we address critical optimization challenges, including Peak-to-Average Power Ratio (PAPR) reduction, self- and mutual-interference management, and adaptive resource allocation tailored to dynamic environments. Finally, the paper outlines future research directions to achieve fully flexible and reconfigurable ISAC systems, serving as a technical roadmap for the development of next-generation perceptive mobile networks.
humanized_abstract:
Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is considered as a key technology for the 5G-Advanced and 6G systems to enhance spectral efficiency and reduce hardware cost. To support the ever-increasing intelligent applications including autonomous vehicles, extended reality, and smart manufacturing, the RF ISAC employs radar-like sensing capabilities to work together with the wireless communication. This article provides the most comprehensive survey on the ISAC signals with three major branches: signal design, signal processing and signal optimization.

This survey starts by exploring design of ISAC signals with the perspective of development of mobile communications, highlighting the corresponding waveforms including Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) and its variants, as well as new waveform candidates such as Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) and Filter-Bank Multi-Carrier (FBMC) for 6G. It then moves on to explore robust signal processing methods that may include design methods based on channel information matrix (CIM), spectral line estimation, high-resolution subspace methods, and other corresponding methods to achieve favorable performance in parameter estimation. Next, the optimization aspects including PAPR reduction, self-interference and mutual-interference management, and adaptive resource optimization according to the scenarios of the system are explored. This survey eventually provides future directions for flexible and reconfigurable ISACs and accordingly serves as a technological roadmap for perceptive mobile networks.
